html:
Elsoft Research is no longer the clean SiC burn-in story described in the prior draft. I found no current filing support for that claim. The better thesis is more contrarian: this is a tiny, ignored Malaysian ATE company whose legacy LED and smart-device test business has collapsed, but whose fixed engineering base is being redeployed into two new niches, peritoneal dialysis embedded systems and metalens test solutions. The market still prices it as a failed old-cycle equipment name, while 2026 already has enough confirmed medical orders to more than cover 2025 group revenue.

At MYR 0.255, Elsoft’s market cap is about MYR 177 million. The 2025 annual report shows RM78.7 million of other investments and RM4.8 million of cash, with no debt, so roughly 47% of the market cap is liquid financial assets. That cash cushion is not by itself a reason to buy, because idle investment income has masked weak operations. The real question is whether the operating business can return to even RM7 million to RM10 million of normalized earnings. If it can, the ex-liquid-assets valuation is only about 9x to 13x forward earnings for a company with high operating leverage and net cash.

The bear case is obvious, which is why the stock is available. FY2025 revenue fell 39% to RM8.4 million, gross profit turned into a RM0.9 million gross loss, and reported PAT of RM5.1 million was flattered by investment gains and other income. TA Research stripped out exceptional items and put FY2025 core profit at only RM0.8 million, then cut its forecasts and downgraded the stock to Hold. Retail context is also washed out: i3Investor discussion is thin and mostly old semicon-cycle chatter, while a 2025 Reddit post framed Elsoft as a business that needed a reboot. That is the setup. This is not a stock being bid for a fashionable narrative.

The forward inflection is real enough to underwrite a speculative thesis. Management says medical devices should become the key 2026 driver, backed by about RM10 million of confirmed orders scheduled for fulfillment this year. The Edge reported that a local medical company ordered 2,000 embedded systems for peritoneal kidney dialysis machines worth RM10 million to RM12 million, double the prior year’s 1,000 systems. That order alone is larger than all FY2025 revenue. If legacy ATE stabilizes at a few million, and metalens testers contribute even two to four systems at over RM1 million each, FY2026 revenue can plausibly move back toward RM16 million to RM22 million.

That matters because Elsoft kept the engineering platform intact. The 2025 annual report shows only 65 employees, including 26 R&D engineers and 15 service engineers. R&D expenses were RM5.2 million in 2025, an extreme 61% of revenue. That looks ugly on trough sales, but it also means incremental revenue drops through quickly if the new products scale. The company already demonstrated this kind of operating torque in prior cycles: revenue of RM28 million to RM29 million in 2021-2022 supported RM11 million to RM43 million of PAT, although 2022 included non-operating help. The clean takeaway is not that Elsoft will regain 2018 economics. It is that a very small revenue swing can radically change reported earnings.

The variant perception is that Elsoft is becoming less dependent on old LED/smart-device ATE and more like a specialized embedded electronics and precision test house. Peritoneal dialysis fits the company’s skill set: embedded controls, reliability, regulated-device supply discipline, and long product qualification cycles. It is not as glamorous as AI semicap, but the demand driver is durable: decentralizing renal care away from hospitals. Metalens testing is higher torque. Management says it is developing test solutions for ultra-slim metalens used in consumer electronics and AR/VR applications, with units priced above RM1 million. The metalens opportunity is earlier and riskier, but a small number of wins is material to a company this size.

The right way to value Elsoft is not trailing P/E. Reported FY2025 earnings were low quality, and trailing revenue is trough revenue. I would use a sum of liquid assets plus forward core earnings power. Start with RM83 million of cash and investments. In a base case, assume FY2026 revenue of RM18 million, gross margin normalizing toward the mid-20s as medical systems absorb fixed cost, and total core PAT around RM6 million to RM7 million before volatile investment gains. Put 18x on RM7 million, because this would still be a small, lumpy, customer-concentrated business, and add most of the liquid asset value. That gets to roughly RM200 million to RM215 million equity value, or MYR 0.29 to MYR 0.31 per share.

The upside case is where this gets interesting. If medical repeats or expands beyond the first 2,000 systems, and metalens test equipment becomes a real product line rather than a pilot, revenue can move back above RM25 million. At that scale, the fixed R&D base becomes an asset again. RM10 million to RM12 million of core PAT is plausible, not heroic. A 20x multiple on RM11 million plus RM70 million to RM80 million of liquid assets gives RM290 million to RM300 million of value, or about MYR 0.42 to MYR 0.45 per share. That is still well below the old-cycle peaks, but it would be a 65% to 75% return from today.

The main risks are customer concentration, execution, and capital allocation. One medical customer accounted for roughly half of FY2025 revenue. If that dialysis order slips, the thesis slips. Metalens may remain a promising science project rather than a repeatable product. The RM20 million planned 2026 spend is a double-edged sword: it can fund the transition without dilution, but it can also turn liquid assets into low-return R&D if management chases too many niches. The dividend was cut to 0.5 sen for FY2025, so the liquid asset balance is not being aggressively returned. This is a transition stock, not a balance-sheet liquidation.

My view: Elsoft is not a high-conviction compounder yet, but it is a credible small-cap inflection with a protected balance sheet and asymmetric operating leverage. The stock is most attractive if Q1 and Q2 2026 confirm that the medical order is converting into revenue and that metalens orders are real, not promotional language.

Current price: MYR 0.255.

Bear target: MYR 0.18, 29% downside. Medical orders disappoint, ATE remains weak, investment gains fade, and the stock trades near tangible asset support with little value for the operating business.

Base target: MYR 0.31, 22% upside. Medical revenue lands, FY2026 core PAT reaches roughly RM6 million to RM7 million, and the market gives modest credit for the transition.

Bull target: MYR 0.45, 76% upside. Medical systems become repeat orders, metalens testers add several million of high-value revenue, core PAT moves toward RM10 million to RM12 million, and the stock rerates from dead ATE name to niche embedded-test platform.
